description:
## Goal

`Monitoring.report_status` (private, in
`src/fetcher/monitoring.cr`) is the body of the periodic
reporter. It reads from four actors:

- `MemoryManagerActor.instance.get_memory_status`
- `SocketManager.instance.connection_count`
- `EventBroadcaster.client_count`
- `FiberTracker.stats`

The method is heavily defensive — each diagnostic counter is
wrapped in its own `begin / rescue`, and the outer body is
also wrapped. The comment at lines 184-186 of the file says
"each diagnostic counter is best-effort: a failure in one
should not silence the others or kill the reporter loop."

This is a real correctness invariant that is not exercised
by any spec. The reporter only runs in production (every
5 minutes via `REPORT_INTERVAL`), so a regression here would
silently degrade observability for the P0 memory-growth
investigation (`quickhea-alz`).

## Why now

The reporter is on the suspect list for `quickhea-alz` — if
the reporter is silently failing in production, we can't
trust the memory-status logs. Having a test that asserts
"if one actor fails, the others still log" gives us a
regression guard for the most important correctness
invariant of the reporter.

## Design

The cleanest approach is a small test seam: extract a
private method that takes the four dependencies as
parameters, and have the public path call it with the
singleton values. Then the spec can call the private
method with mocks/stubs:

```crystal
private def self.report_status : Nil
  memory_status = read_memory_status
  socket_count = read_socket_count
  event_clients = read_event_clients
  fiber_stats = read_fiber_stats
  log_memory_status(memory_status, socket_count, event_clients, fiber_stats)
end

private def self.read_memory_status
  MemoryManagerActor.instance.get_memory_status
end

private def self.read_socket_count
  SocketManager.instance.connection_count
rescue ex : Exception
  Log.for("quickheadlines.memory").debug(exception: ex) { "socket_count unavailable" }
  0
end

# ... etc
```

Then the spec can replace the `read_*` methods via a
module-level hook (e.g., a class variable that defaults to
the method but can be overridden in tests), or by directly
calling the lower-level helpers.

A simpler alternative: the test can stub the `SocketManager`
singleton to raise, call `Monitoring.start`, and verify the
fiber does not die. This requires less plumbing.

## Acceptance

- The invariant "one failing counter does not silence the
  others" has a regression test
- Full suite passes
- `just nix-build` succeeds
title: Improve testability of Monitoring.report_status
